subject: Economics
chapter: Employment: Growth, Informalisation and Other Issues

questions.q: What is the main cause of informalization in the labour market?
questions.answer: Informalization in the labour market can be attributed to the lack of job security, low wages, and lack of social benefits provided by formal employment.

questions.q: Define the concept of unemployment in an economy. Explain its causes and effects.
questions.answer: Unemployment is a situation in which individuals capable of working and willing to do so at prevailing wages and working conditions are unable to find employment. Causes of unemployment include technological changes, globalisation, and economic slowdown. Effects of unemployment include poverty, inequality, and social unrest.

questions.q: What is the main difference between open unemployment and disguised unemployment?
questions.answer: Open unemployment is when a person is capable of working but has no job, while disguised unemployment is when a person is working but not contributing to the production process.

questions.q: Explain the concept of informal sector and its characteristics.
questions.answer: The informal sector is a part of the economy that operates outside the formal regulatory framework. Characteristics of the informal sector include lack of job security, low wages, and no social benefits.

questions.q: What is the main cause of jobless growth in an economy?
Explain the concept of underemployment in an economy. Explain its causes and effects.
questions.answer: Underemployment is a situation in which individuals are working less than their desired hours or are not using their skills and abilities to their full potential. Causes of underemployment include technological changes, globalisation, and lack of job opportunities. Effects of underemployment include poverty, inequality, and reduced economic growth.

questions.q: What is the main difference between voluntary unemployment and involuntary unemployment?
questions.answer: Voluntary unemployment is when a person chooses not to work, while involuntary unemployment is when a person is unable to find a job despite being willing to work.

questions.q: Explain the concept of disguised unemployment in an economy. Explain its causes and effects.
questions.answer: Disguised unemployment is a situation in which individuals are working but not contributing to the production process. Causes of disguised unemployment include lack of modern technology and inefficient production methods. Effects of disguised unemployment include reduced economic growth and poverty.

questions.q: What is the main cause of seasonal unemployment in an economy?
Explain the concept of open unemployment in an economy. Explain its causes and effects.
questions.answer: Open unemployment is a situation in which individuals are capable of working but have no job. Causes of open unemployment include technological changes, globalisation, and economic slowdown. Effects of open unemployment include poverty, inequality, and social unrest.
